content_of_post_to_answer: Why do certain life events change how we view and celebrate our birthdays?
answer: Birthdays often change meaning because they stop being about the number and start reflecting what that year has taught us. A person who has faced loss, parenthood, illness, achievement, or major change may celebrate differently because time is no longer an abstract idea. The hidden shift is from counting years lived to recognizing experiences survived, relationships built, and versions of ourselves we have outgrown. A birthday can become less a celebration of age and more a marker of identity.
